currentState: Chapter 8 turns their fast field alignment into proven city-side partnership. Havel provides the district read and the fallback logic; Sylvanas provides the pressure, the capture, and the speed that turns local pattern into decisive action.
powerDynamic: Sylvanas still owns command and kill-space judgment. Havel owns the lived map of Andorhal and the knowledge of what the city can still hide. The chapter proves their abilities mesh cleanly under immediate pressure.

physicalDynamics.pattern: They stand at the window over the loading yards reading the same men for different reasons until the two views click into one target picture.
They spend the chapter moving through the same city at the same tempo: Havel opening doors and shaping exits, Sylvanas taking bodies and deciding direction before the door swings shut behind them.

nameUsage.sylvanasCallsHavel: Direct surname use continues, now with the practical trust of a commander who has seen the local captain's escape logic hold under fire.
nameUsage.havelCallsSylvanas: Still rank-light and blunt, but now fully settled into competent mutual shorthand.

history.event: Havel gives Sylvanas the first usable human map of the granary district, including Teren, the sober brothers, and the wrong quiet hours.
Together they identify caches, covert discipline, and the minimum size of the embedded cell, turning Havel from ignored witness into trusted local ally.
Havel identifies the target and gives the trap its working lane; Sylvanas makes the snatch succeed.
The witness death and the closing net test their alignment immediately, and Havel's fallback logic meshes cleanly with Sylvanas's pursuit reading.
By chapter end they accept the right division cleanly: Sylvanas takes the moving hunt forward, Havel holds the city behind with false trails and split evidence.

trajectory: Outsider militia witness -> local partner in the hunt -> proven city-side ally whose value is strongest when left in place.

stateHistory.currentState: Fast field alignment built on mutual pattern-reading. Havel knows the district; Sylvanas knows what disciplined cover looks like. Neither wastes time pretending the other needs a gentler version of the truth.
